description: Trigger discrimination for the compliance cluster: the 27001 ISMS projection vs the AIMS, SOC 2 scoping, the SoA document, the control catalog, gap assessment, and technical threat modeling.

cases.prompt: Prepare us for ISO 27001 — management system design, internal audit program, management review, and how our existing controls slot into Annex A.
cases.reason: Clause 4–10 ISMS design with Annex A selection is the 27001 projection; the AIMS is a different standard and where-do-we-stand is the gap auditor.

cases.prompt: Stand up the management system for our AI systems specifically — AI risk assessments and AI impact assessments included.
cases.reason: The AIMS with 6.1.2/6.1.4 AI machinery is ISO 42001's projection, even though the clause skeleton is harmonized with 27001.

cases.prompt: US enterprise customers keep asking for our SOC 2 report — plan what it takes.
cases.reason: SOC 2 is an AICPA attestation scoped by TSC categories and Type — no ISMS, no SoA; it has its own projection skill.

cases.prompt: Write the Statement of Applicability from our completed risk treatment plan.
cases.reason: The SoA document is authored by its own skill; the ISMS architect designs the 6.1 machinery that feeds it.

cases.prompt: Audit our current state against ISO 27001 and rank what's missing.
cases.reason: Current-state assessment with prioritized gaps is the parameterized gap audit; the architect designs the target system.

cases.prompt: Model the threats against our file-upload pipeline so we know what risks to register.
cases.reason: Technical threat enumeration is the Phase 4 skill; the ISMS consumes it as clause 6 risk-assessment input.
